kernel_of_truth.summary: All actionable content of the 11:57Z poll has been resolved by intervening decider work between this poll and the current analyst run. PRED-105's tacit-withdrawal threshold breach was anticipated by the prior analyst run's pre-staged proposal, picked up by decider, integrated as EXP-284, ISS-1800 closed and verified. The substantive fingerprint of the poll is therefore not a new actionable item — it is the continuing, hardening pattern of pipeline-frozen-while-cron-alive on the dome side.
kernel_of_truth.detail: Charitable read first: the dome's auto-pipeline did fire HOLD #11 daily-AI-review on 2026-05-02T07:08:24Z (b73722c, 27min earlier than the predicted 07:35Z window), and the file daily_review.json did update — but only the timestamp/eclipse_days fields. The registry block (confirmed=69, refined=4, prospective=10, registry_ratio_pct=94.5) is now byte-stable across two consecutive HOLD-fire events (HOLD #10 on 2026-05-01, HOLD #11 today). This is the second consecutive event of the signature 'cron alive, content updated on non-registry fields, registry mutation skipped.' One such event is consistent with a transient — a single failed registry write or a brief manual-disable. Two consecutive events at the daily cadence raises the probability that the registry-update step is now wired behind an explicit gate (manual approval) or has been removed from the auto-pipeline entirely. A third consecutive event at HOLD #12 (~07:35Z 2026-05-03) would, per the poller's own thresholding rule, make 'manual-approval-gate' the strong default. We respect that thresholding instead of pre-empting it.

pred_105_assessment.counter_argument: The poller's HIGH-severity sub-flag was raised at 11:57Z; verdict advancement happened at 13:16Z (~80min later via decider integration of the pre-staged proposal). This is exactly the workflow the prior analyst run anticipated and pre-staged — the HIGH sub-flag was 'actionable' at poll time but had a defined consumer in the queue. Counter: a stricter agent might argue the HIGH sub-flag should be re-raised here as a formal acknowledgement of the threshold breach, not just left implicit in the integrated artifacts. Defense: re-raising would create work that duplicates EXP-284; the artifact landed correctly and is verified. The right action is to acknowledge OBE here in writing (this record) so the audit trail shows the chain handled the threshold breach end-to-end rather than dropping it.

manual_commit_drought_milestone.significance: FIRST 15d+ reading on the public record. The 14d threshold (which equals the prediction-window length for PRED-105) was crossed yesterday; the 15d threshold (~107% of the prediction-window length) is crossed today exactly 5min after the 11:57Z poll. Pattern (a) 'sustained do-not-adjudicate as infrastructure' continues to gain evidence; pattern (b) 'imminent batched manual edit' is not ruled out but each polling cycle without a manual commit shrinks its prior. Conservative call (mirroring poller): no immediate patch action. Continue tracking. The author-contact prior is not invoked here — there is no published surface change to attribute as 'responsive' or 'not responsive'; absence of activity is itself the data point.
falsification_signatures_to_watch: A multi-PRED + WIN-registry commit signed Nick Hughes (not GitHub Action) on the next ~24-72h would be the pattern-(b) gate-release signature; we revise verdicts at that point.
Continued silence past d=22 (~one full prediction-window past the 8 EOM PREDs' closure) makes pattern (a) near-certain by the poller's stated rule.
Any source-file touch (monitor.py, scripts/pull_data.py, scripts/build_daily_review.py, inject_ai_layer.py, verify_predictions.py, generate_manifest.js) is CRITICAL — pipeline-architecture change.
Any new .ots file timestamping a predictions-only document (separate from status_history.json) → CANARY TRIGGERED.

hold_without_registry_update_pattern.decision_rule: If HOLD #12 fires AND skips registry-update, the streak hits 3 — manual-approval-gate becomes the strong default per the poller's stated thresholding rule. At that point we should consider proposing a section-level addendum to Section 5.16 (monitoring infrastructure) documenting the registry-update gating event. We do NOT propose that addendum now — one more event is the principled threshold.
hold_without_registry_update_pattern.counter_argument: The 2-event streak could be incidental — the author may be in mid-refactor of the registry-update step (e.g., switching from automatic to manual-approval) and the gating is transient editing, not structural. Defense: the source files (build_daily_review.py specifically) have unchanged SHAs across 5+ polls, so no editing is happening on the public surface; the registry-update gating is being effected without source-file modification. That's consistent with either (a) deliberate runtime gate via config or environment, or (b) deliberate gate via a separate manual-trigger workflow not visible in the source tree. Both interpretations leave the dome's reluctance to adjudicate as the operative observation.

author_contact_prior_application.rationale: Per CLAUDE.md author-contact discipline (added 2026-04-27): the prior shifts but does not make everything responsive. This poll observes ZERO new published-surface content (all 6 page hashes byte-identical, no manual commits, all 10 commits are auto-pipeline). There is nothing to attribute as 'responsive' or 'not responsive.' Author-contact prior is therefore not actionable on this poll. The dome's absence of public-surface change is itself the data; we interpret it under the existing pattern-(a)-vs-(b) framework, not under the responsiveness frame.
